svg.aria-label: The DOS coordination payoff: a stale write clobbering a cancellation, then prevented. Two live agents act on one shared reservation NM1VX1, each having planned its tool-calls against the same original state, neither aware of the other. A1 cancels the reservation and the DB-hash moves to a3f1…afterA1. Under NAIVE replay, A2's add-bag — computed on the original active state — blindly re-activates the reservation and adds a bag, silently overwriting A1's cancellation; the composed hash 77c2…naive is a real lost update. Under the ARBITER, dos.arbiter leases the region reservations/NM1VX1 to A1, refuses A2's overlapping lease until A1 releases, and A2 re-plans against the post-A1 cancelled state and correctly declines — the DB-hash matches the serialized-correct value a3f1…serial and no update is lost. Across six natural conflict pairs drawn from the real task distribution, J = 4 of 6 clobbers were structurally prevented off the DB-hash.
svg.title: The coordination payoff — a stale write clobbers a cancellation under naive, prevented by the arbiter
svg.desc: Two agents on shared reservation NM1VX1 planned against the same original state. A1 cancels (hash a3f1…afterA1). Naive: A2's stale add-bag re-activates the reservation and clobbers the cancel (hash 77c2…naive — a real lost update). Arbiter: A2's overlapping lease on reservations/NM1VX1 is refused; A2 re-plans on the cancelled state and declines; hash matches serialized-correct a3f1…serial. J=4/6 clobbers prevented, conflicts drawn from the real 2.35% natural pairwise rate.
svg.text: — the coordination payoff: a stale write, clobbering then prevented
Two live agents act on one shared reservation. Neither knows the other exists.
Both planned their tool-calls against the same original state. Watch what happens when their effects land on one DB — naive, then under the arbiter.

g.text: Task: add a checked bag to NM1VX1.
also computed against the ORIGINAL DB — it never saw A1
SHARED DB — reservation NM1VX1 (after A1 cancels)
A2's add-bag was computed on the ORIGINAL (active) state, so a
blind replay RE-ACTIVATES the reservation and adds a bag —
hash 77c2…naive ≠ serialized-correct · a real lost update
dos.arbiter leases reservations/NM1VX1 to A1. A2's concurrent
lease on the SAME region OVERLAPS → refused until A1 releases.
A2 re-plans on the cancelled state and correctly declines.
hash a3f1…serial ✓ · no lost update · J += 1
The whole fleet result — 6 natural conflict pairs, live, J off the DB-hash:
clobbers structurally prevented — the arbiter refused the second lease on reservations/<id> each time.
Conflicts were DRAWN FROM the real task distribution (2.35% natural pairwise rate, 18 sites), not pinned — which kills the "constructed conflict" objection.
